taxonomy: Zosterophyllanthos rectipetalus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. It belongs to the genus Zosterophyllanthos, which consists of several species of plants native to Australia. As a member of the Euphorbiaceae family, Zosterophyllanthos rectipetalus is closely related to other plants with similar characteristics, such as spurge and poinsettia. The plant's classification is based on its morphological and anatomical features, which are used to determine its taxonomic position. By placing Zosterophyllanthos rectipetalus within the Euphorbiaceae family, botanists have been able to understand its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants.
